title: Rejections carry literal next calls
description: possible_repairs and did_you_mean as complete, paste-ready op JSON, never prose advice or bare names. Copying beats composing for weak emitters; this is the constrained decoder for stacks without grammars. Partially done (repairs work started); finish to the executable-verbatim bar. cross-model.md engine #1.
notes:
Chunk 1 done: Repair type, view addressing-miss repairs (pkg/sym/receiver/method/field cases), executable-verbatim filter, daemon wire plumbing, spec updated. Next chunks: patch-op addressing misses, stale-generation view repair, unknown-op substitution, help-call fallback for shape errors.
Chunks 2-3 done: stale-generation view repair, unknown-op corrected-patch repair (no repair on catalog fallback), unknown-handle view repair, envelope sym-miss view repairs. Remaining: in-op symbol substitution (rename/set_body targeting missing syms), position/kind keyword substitution, help-call fallback for op-shape errors, init.go teaching text mentions possible_repairs.
Chunks 4-6 done: where/with keyword substitution, in-op sym/pkg addressing substitution with resolution filter, init.go teaches paste-the-repair. Remaining for close: single-op sugar paths (rename/set_body/add_param direct calls carry no repairs yet), help-call fallback for op-shape errors, semantic-diagnostic repairs (undefined ident near-miss).
All planned classes done: sugar paths (rename/set_body/add_param), query tier, help fallback for missing required args, undefined-ident search repairs at all seven typecheck reject sites. Deliberately no repair: semantic guesses beyond search, upsert_decl pkg miss (creates packages on demand), catalog-dump fallbacks. Close after a bench round proves repair uptake.

title: Authoring driver: ago agent verb, engine tool wiring, config profiles
description: ADR 0005. cmd/ago: agent verb running one-shot episodes; Tools implementation multiplexing mcpCall (ten ago tools) with FileTools read_file/write_file; ToolDefs generated from mcpTools schemas plus the two file tools; system prompt evolved from bench promptSemantic; profiles (endpoint, model, sampler, key env) in .ago/agent.json with --profile/--endpoint/--model flags; JSONL transcript to .ago/sessions/.
close_reason: Landed on authoring branch; dogfood run against qwen is the remaining proof

title: Authoring driver: OpenAI-compat HTTP client against httptest fake
description: ADR 0005. internal/agent client implementing Complete over /v1/chat/completions with native tool calling: request shaping (messages, tools, tool_choice auto), tool_calls argument decode (JSON string to map), retry with backoff on 429/5xx honoring Retry-After. TDD against httptest; llama-swap router glm/qwen are the real targets.
close_reason: Landed on authoring branch

title: Authoring driver milestone 1: client interface + one-shot loop with scripted fake model
description: ADR 0005. internal/agent: one-method Client interface (OpenAI-compat chat completions with tool calling), one-shot loop with step/wall caps and JSONL transcript, tool dispatch behind an interface so the engine wiring lands separately. TDD: loop behavior proven against a scripted fake model before any HTTP client exists.
close_reason: Loop and file tools landed test-first on authoring branch (d6cf46c, 92a2810); Client/Tools interfaces proven against scripted fakes

title: Move miner: collisions, move+rename compounds, correct rejects
description: tasks-move.json is 0 for 24. Diagnosis 2026-07-16: b966215e and 5026ca97 class are English moves, correctly rejected. Real misses: (1) parseDecls keeps first occurrence per bare name across all changed files, so cross package name collisions pair wrong files; (2) move plus rename compounds (vault 45b0179a: mergeStates to MergeReplicationStates, compareStates to compareReplicationStates) need body similarity pairing and a compound spec {pkg, sym, to_pkg, to_name} plus oracle recipe move_decl then rename; (3) whole package moves (9733a250 A:49, b58dada4 A:130) likely out of scope, reject with named reason. Move family is where the protocol structurally beats LSP tools; bench cannot show that with zero certified moves.
notes: Miner fixed and committed (72358c2, 4b8a374): 5 tasks / 14 specs / 5 compound, every reject names a true reason. Remaining move blockers are engine ceilings, all beaded: 6yh multi-module (blocks 7ec1fe75, 45b0179a), la8 importFallback (dd2c3807), ue9 grouped decls (b26814a3), 3x7 external test pkgs (687dd1bd).
close_reason: extraction correct and honest; zero mystery rejects; remaining work lives in the four engine ceiling beads

title: Invalid-intermediate rate metric
description: Pass/fail measures outcome; the mechanism claim is that invalid edits are unrepresentable. Metric: how often an arm writes non compiling code to disk at ANY point during an episode. ago is zero by construction, raw is high, serena is the middle. Design: watch worktree writes during episode (fsnotify or per step git status hash) and typecheck on change, or cheaper proxy: typecheck at every agent step boundary from the opencode event stream. Record per episode in episodes.jsonl and MLflow.
close_reason: Landed: watchIntermediates samples .go mtime fingerprints, debounces one interval, runs go build ./... per settled state, final state always sampled. Fields in episodes.jsonl, report column (invalid mid-states), MLflow metric keys. Oracle control episode: 1 state, 0 invalid.

title: Serena as third bench arm: symbols without validation
description: Raw vs semantic conflates symbol addressing with mandatory validation. Serena (LSP/gopls MCP) has symbol addressing but advisory validation: probe showed it returns OK while writing type errors gopls could see. Adding it as a bench mode gives the ablation between the two variables with an off the shelf tool. Work: serena mode in bench (opencode MCP config for serena, no shell), prompt parity, scoring unchanged. Report semantic vs serena vs raw.
close_reason: Serena arm landed (94ed3a9 + smoke evidence): third mode with semantic-equivalent lockdown, pinned serena rev, shell and memory tools excluded, prompt teaches the live tool surface. Smoke: rename passes, move/add-param fail, and the invalid-intermediate metric captures the advisory-validation gap (3/3 broken states in one episode, 0 in the passing one). Frozen grid is 3 models x raw/serena/semantic.

title: importFallback: nameless stub for fresh test variant import
description: boundary_dd2c3807, after create_pkg plus test-file landing plus import carry all work: final reject is 'could not import github.com/hashicorp/go-kms-wrapping/v2/extras/crypto (invalid package name: )' in the freshly created libs/crypto agent_test.go. importFallback either matched a shallow stub in some package's Imports() (empty Name) or the on-demand packages.Load returned nameless. Reproduce: AGO_BENCH_MODES=oracle sweep boundary_dd2c3807; inspect which arm returned the package. Fix direction: skip empty-Name matches in the workspace scan and fall through to on-demand load; or force full reload when a created file imports a path new to its package. Evidence: run 2026-07-16 ~15:49 local.

title: Held-out task set
description: Against adaptive overfitting: mine tasks from repos untouched during development (etcd, caddy, hugo candidates), oracle-certify AFTER the evaluation freeze, run the frozen grid on them, report dev-set and held-out results side by side. The miners make this one clone + one command per repo.
notes: Candidates mined and committed pre-tag (etcd 322, caddy 28, hugo 102). Deliberately stopped before extraction: spec derivation and oracle certification run post-tag with the frozen engine so no held-out task can influence development. Post-tag: prep-rename/addparam/move over candidates-{etcd,caddy,hugo}, oracle sweep, certify, frozen grid.

title: Evaluation freeze protocol
description: Paper prerequisite: declare all runs to date development data. When the grid is ready (3 models x 2 modes + ablation + effort cells), tag a release and re-run the full grid on the frozen tag; only frozen-tag runs feed the paper's headline tables. ago_rev already stamps every run so the phases are auditable. Write the dev-phase/freeze distinction into the paper's methodology - the bench-driven-fixes loop (repairs, discovery primitives) is a documented contribution of the development phase, not contamination.
notes: All three tag gates closed (uj8, pna, kxa). Freeze tag now blocks only on serving readiness for the grid (mrc): glm UD-Q4_K_XL and qwen udq4 servers, then tag and re-run.

title: Repairs ablation arm
description: For the paper: AGO_NO_REPAIRS env (daemon strips possible_repairs and escalation from responses) so a round can isolate the repair channel's contribution. The natural experiment exists (episode 20260715-212439 before/after defenses: 75 flailing steps to 22 productive) but a controlled arm is the publishable version.

title: Milestone: arXiv paper
description: Publish the research: compiler-validated semantic edit protocol for weak local coding models. Paper skeleton (thesis, protocol design incl. the repair conversation, oracle methodology, bench results, released artifact under MIT). Data prerequisites: qwen floor round (in flight), one more model (gpt-oss-20b per roster), repair-uptake cross-model analysis, repairs-ablation arm. All figures and tables GENERATED from episodes.jsonl/MLflow per tenet 1 - no hand-made numbers. Note: first-time arXiv submitters in cs.SE may need endorsement.

title: inspect on a type lists its methods
description: Round 20260715-223716 autopsy: GLM lost 4/4 episodes on vault_cfff8d42 hunting a method of the unexported backend type - 40 method-not-found rejects, no discovery move available. inspect {pkg, sym} on a type should list its method set (name + signature per method). Then method-or-field-not-found repairs point at the inspect call. Complements the package outline bead (agent-go-944) one level down.

title: Milestone: project ops
description: The missing family between refactoring language and coding language: add_dependency, remove_dependency, move_file, delete_file, mod_tidy (agent-go-9mg), plus new-file creation inside composable patches (lifts upsert and move_decl ceilings, agent-go-dp6). Design note: file ops change the package graph - lean on the post-edit-edges scheduler.
notes: delete_file, move_file, add/remove_dependency, mod_tidy shipped; upsert_decl new-file and new-package ceilings lifted. Remaining: none of the named ops. Language.md project ops table now fully shipped.
close_reason: all named ops shipped: new file and new package in composable patches, delete_file, move_file, add_dependency, remove_dependency, mod_tidy, move_decl create_pkg with repair; catalog v6, surface.md and language.md current; follow-on ceilings split into their own beads

title: Milestone: qwen3.5-9b floor round
description: Profile added (bench/profiles.json qwen3.5-9b, endpoint qwen.lab.aeryx.ai). Roster floor cell: smoke one episode first, then k=3 on certified tasks, scored separately (raw near-certain zero; any semantic pass is the strongest single datapoint). Runs as soon as the GLM round frees local compute; endpoint is separate so it could even overlap if Guy okays the load.
close_reason: round complete and exported: semantic 25/33 (75%) vs raw 15/33 (45%) at k=3 plus sizing; raw goes 0/4 on five of eleven tasks where semantic scores 2-4/4; evidence 20260716-132637, MLflow experiment agent-go-bench

title: Milestone: round readout + cfff8d42 autopsy
description: When run 20260715-223716 completes: bench report, commit evidence, write the comparison analysis. Priority within: autopsy vault_cfff8d42 (oracle-certified, raw 4/4, semantic 0/4 capped) from its request logs - the purest usability signal we have. Findings feed prompts, repairs, or ergonomics.
close_reason: GLM round reported (semantic 32/44 vs raw 28/44), evidence committed, ANALYSIS.md written; cfff8d42 autopsy done: gopls closure-name prior found, inspect now lists methods, method-miss inspect repair and funcN redirect shipped

title: add_param: manage imports on the declaration file
description: Oracle sweep: add-param of ctx context.Context to boundary vault credential_store.go rejected 'undefined: context' - add_param splices the param type into a file without the import. upsert_decl already runs imports.Process; add_param (and its composable op) must too, on every touched file. Repro: task boundary_b4b95e0f.
notes: Two fixes: imports.Process on every touched file in AddParam, and the deeper one - retypecheck's stdlib fallback importer was per-package, so two files gaining the same new import got two incompatible type universes. Now one snapshot-shared cache, graph-first. Verified through the real CLI path.

title: Daemon request log: -log flag + JSONL in handle()
description: General daemon option, off by default: op, args digest, outcome, rejection reason, repairs offered, generation, latency per request as JSONL. Must reach the auto-spawned daemon (dial() and MCP command paths); env var simplest. Unlocks op-mix, resubmission, repair-uptake, stale-gen, time-to-first-mutation counters. Proposal section 3.

title: Serving profiles: bench/profiles.json + loader
description: Profile struct: endpoint, model id, sampler (repeat penalty explicitly 1.0), thinking/effort, quant label, context size, notes. Load in setup(), embed whole profile in run.json, res[profile] per episode. Server identity (build probe, model hash) folds in here. Sub-item: sampler wiring into opencode provider options. Proposal section 2.

title: failure_kind episode taxonomy
description: Replace agent_error bool with failure_kind: capped|endpoint_error|harness_crash|tool_parse|scored_fail. classifyFailure(agentErr, timedOut, agentOut, pass) in bench_test.go episode(); derive markers from real transcripts under bench/results/. Proposal section 1.

title: Rework bench to match framework proposal
description: Bring bench/ in line with docs/specs/bench-framework-proposal.md: taxonomy, per-model profiles, daemon evidence, oracle arm, both harnesses first-class (opencode + Pi via MCP extension), MLflow derived comparison layer, log placement and token accounting per the resolved proposal. Break into small chunks; delta map from Explore agent drives the order.
close_reason: proposal implemented: failure taxonomy, pinned profiles, per-episode daemon request logs, oracle arm with certified gating, token accounting from step_finish events, prompt parity test, test-gate baseline, Wilson CIs, MLflow export; the Pi harness axis (js2.13) stays open as its own item

title: Decl ops: move_decl, set_signature, remove_param, implement_interface, add_bench
description: Deferred from the language plan. set_signature rejects with the call-site list unless sites are repaired in the same patch (spec line 102). implement_interface and add_bench round out the decl tier.
notes:
Priority raised: 24 mined move tasks and 1 signature task in bench/tasks.json are blocked on move_decl and set_signature. These ops are the path to complex bench cases beyond rename/add-param.
Oracle sweep 20260715: five more tasks proven blocked on set_signature-class ops - func-as-value (fb1ea92a, 7103bc2c), variadic spread call sites (bf1486f7, eb61ac63), interface+implementors atomic change (9354a4eb). With the 24 move tasks, 30 of 46 mined tasks now wait on this issue.
set_signature SHIPPED (6b3f618, 592c448): name-matched param carry with defaults, spread-site insertion, interface methods; interface+implementors verified as one atomic patch. add_param's spread ceiling also removed directly (f11f7c0). Remaining in this issue: move_decl, remove_param, implement_interface, add_bench.
move_decl SHIPPED (1d030e6): self-contained decls, reference requalification, explicit module-local imports. Move bench vertical shipped (e356d47): extractor mined 4/24 clean single-decl moves, oracle replays via move_decl patches. Remaining ops: remove_param, implement_interface, add_bench.
close_reason: move_decl and set_signature shipped and certified through the oracle (interface plus implementors atomic patches, value-use compositions); the three remaining stubs split into their own P3 bead

title: Run the bench roster
description: 10 cells per cross-model.md roster table. Fill order when time-boxed: GLM (wired), gpt-oss-20b, Qwen3.6-27B, gpt-oss-120b, Coder-Next, Gemma 4, DeepSeek, Devstral pair, 8B floor last. 8B cell scored separately (raw mode near-certain zero; smoke one episode before committing a k). Gemma 4 is the generalist arm, reversing the README exclusion. Qwen 8B/27B/80B forms the within-family scale curve.

title: Episode counters in JSONL
description: Tool-call parse failures, JSON decode failures (and whether lenient repair fixed them), op mix with per-op accept/reject, identical-resubmission count, tokens in/out per turn, time to first accepted mutation. Only way to see which optimizations matter per family. cross-model.md bench #2.

title: Per-model serving profiles
description: One named profile per model: parser, template revision, sampler, quant, thinking/effort, grammar on/off. Pinned in manifest, recorded in episode metadata. Repetition penalty off as lineup default. cross-model.md bench #1.
notes: Duplicate of agent-go-js2.2, shipped in 9deb4fa.

title: Determinism as a tested invariant
description: Identical query against unchanged snapshot returns identical bytes: sorted keys, stable ordering, no timestamps or volatile counters. Prefix caching on every stack hangs on this. Test: run every query twice against the fixture, assert byte equality. view_test.go has one determinism check; extend to all queries. cross-model.md engine #3.
notes: Test found two real map-order nondeterminisms (references, funcUses) affecting refs/callers output and edit application order; both sorted at the source.

title: Bounded list responses
description: refs/callers/search capped with total count and explicit truncation marker, pageable. Keeps a popular symbol from dumping 400 sites into a weak model's context. Partial truncation exists in snapshot.go; verify caps, count, marker, paging. cross-model.md engine #4.

title: Accept responses carry fresh {generation, view}
description: Mutations return {generation, view} for the touched declaration so edit-then-edit-again needs no view call between patches. Cheapest structural token win. cross-model.md engine #2.

title: set_body has no import management; models plant dummy functions to justify new imports
description: gpt-oss-120b dogfood episode (dogfood .ago/sessions/20260719-141841.jsonl): set_body bodies using strings.ToLower needed the strings import; the model added 'func _dummy() { _ = strings.ToUpper("") }' to make the import legal. upsert_decl manages imports already; set_body (and likely other body/stmt ops) should get the same treatment: add missing stdlib/module imports when the new body references them, or reject naming the missing import so a repair can offer it.

title: rename op leaves doc comment leading identifier stale
description: First ago agent dogfood episode: rename Greet to Hello updated the decl and all references but left '// Greet returns a greeting for name.' intact. Go convention ties the doc comment's leading word to the decl name; rename should rewrite it (same treatment gopls gives). Repro in any package: rename a documented func, doc keeps the old name.

title: Semantic arm shows 5 invalid mid-states in frozen qwen round; expected 0 by construction
description: Run 20260718-162906: boundary_bf1486f7 iter2 (3/5), boundary_eb61ac63 iter0 (1/3), vault_caec65a7 iter0 (1/2). Semantic writes are typechecked before landing, so on-disk invalid states should be impossible. Suspects: opencode built-in edit tool leaking past the ago-only prompt, go.mod/go.sum transient states during add_dependency restore, or watcher sampling during ago's own atomic write window. Pull the three transcripts and attribute each invalid sample before the paper claims zero-by-construction.
close_reason: Attributed by replay: all 10 accepted add_param ops from boundary_bf1486f7/semantic/2 replayed against the pristine base SHA with go build ./... between each; every settled state builds green. The invalid samples are the within-op multi-file write window (62-88 call-site files written sequentially per accepted op; the watcher fingerprints a half-written state). No host tool leakage (transcripts show zero non-ago tools), no validation failure. Follow-up filed for shrinking the window.

title: Reconcile fallback for compound movers: vault_7ec1fe75
description: 7ec1fe75 movers reject one-at-a-time on unexported cross-package references (same class 687dd1bd had before the reconcile). The reconcile fallback currently only covers the batched path; compound/queued movers (ToName or per-cell singles) keep the old sequential replay and fail. Extend the fallback to the queue path, reusing the rewritten-mover drop rule. Evidence: bench/results/20260717-040924/vault_7ec1fe75.
notes: Compound-mover fallback and grouped members both landed (5f3fd73, e90c406). 7ec1fe75's sole remaining blocker: the commit adds consts to the vendored sdk (vendor/github.com/hashicorp/vault/sdk/helper/consts) and main-module code references them; vendored packages are not primary-addressable, so the reconcile skips them and the end-of-list typecheck rejects with undefined: consts.RequestForwardingALPN. Same workspace-boundary question as 45b0179a; blocked on agent-go-6yh.

title: Audit log UX: live view over the daemon request log
description: The primary use case is everyday local LLM dev against ago; the bench is our instrument, not the user's. The UX reads the daemon request log as its only required source; bench evidence is a bonus view we can link for ourselves, never a dependency. Prerequisite that falls out: request logging is currently opt in (AGO_LOG_REQUESTS, set by the bench harness), so ordinary sessions produce nothing. Flip to on by default: per workspace log under the ago cache dir next to the socket, size capped rotation, opt OUT env for the privacy conscious. Then the UX: live tail of the request stream (op, outcome, latency, rejection reason, repairs offered vs taken, resend escalations) via ago audit serving a local page, TUI variant second, both off one stream reader. Event driven updates (tail the file or a daemon event socket), never polling. Standalone service only if it outgrows the binary; then rookery.
design: No config system. Hard defaults, git reflog precedent: log lives next to the socket in the cache dir (derived, not configurable), size capped rotation keeping current plus one file (~10MB each, bounded ~20MB per workspace), retention IS the rotation, opt out via AGO_NO_REQUEST_LOG=1. Size based rotation only, never time based. AGO_LOG_DIR gets added the day someone has a real reason, not before. Config files stay at zero; 'nothing to start or configure' is a product claim the README makes.
notes: reframed 2026-07-16: daemon log is the product surface, bench optional; default-on logging is the prerequisite. Aggregation is the user's job: longer retention or centralization means tailing the JSONL into their own stack; we ship no exporters, shippers, or hooks. The JSONL format is the integration surface: when the feature lands, the record schema gets documented in the protocol docs (drift guarded like every other wire shape) and nothing else grows around it.

title: Move tier: type plus methods closure move
description: boundary_687dd1bd rejects 'symbol not found': TestSessionConnectionCleanupJob lives in controller's EXTERNAL test package (package controller_test), whose PkgPath differs from the primary, so findObject(controller, sym) misses it. Needs findObject (and move_decl landing rules) to cover external test variants; landing in the target should create name_test-clause files. Evidence: bench/results/20260715-232920/boundary_687dd1bd.
notes: 687dd1bd oracle PASSES (run 20260717-051x, evidence committed): predicate+typecheck green, tests at baseline (boundary DB tests fail on pristine parent too). Landed: (1) retypecheck names import cycles, splice/importFallback race fix (5b67c8f); (2) upsert_decl test-variant replace, agent_test.go landing, imports arg (6607589); (3) composable-patch ledger set: append-to-existing-file landing, delete_decl syms batch + ledger-aware guard, superseding edit merge (fixes the 'found rn' corruption class); (4) fork-universe import fallback (the 'cannot use X as X' splits); (5) bench reconcile: rejected pure batch retries as one atomic patch lifted decl-by-decl from git show; rewritten movers = delete+upsert, unchanged movers = move_decl. Full oracle sweep for regressions in flight; then bench certify 687dd1bd and close. Ceilings noted in reconcile.go doc: grouped multi-spec decls, external test packages, anonymous decls, cross-pkg delete cycles.
close_reason: 687dd1bd certified from oracle evidence (run 20260717-040924). Closure move engine plus the reconcile recipe landed; remaining move tasks tracked separately (agent-go-6yh nested modules, new issue for compound movers).

title: Move tier: grouped decl spec extraction
description: boundary_b26814a3 rejects 'declaration not found': RecoveryUserId lives in a grouped const block in perms; findDeclNode intentionally skips GenDecls with multiple specs. Moving one spec means deleting the spec line from the group (collapsing the group when it empties) and materializing a standalone const in the target. Same ceiling class as upsert_decl grouped replace (documented in UpsertDecl). Evidence: bench/results/20260715-232920/boundary_b26814a3.
close_reason: grouped spec extraction shipped and oracle certified on boundary b26814a3; iota and inherited-value specs reject named

title: Multi-module workspaces: cross module move targets
description: vault_7ec1fe75 moves two ALPN consts from vault to sdk/helper/consts. sdk is a nested module: not loaded as a workspace primary, so move_decl rejects 'package exists but did not load' (correctly, after the clobber guard). Certifying this task needs the snapshot to load a multi-module workspace (go.work style: root plus nested modules with replace directives) so nested module packages are first class move targets. Design question: one Snapshot spanning modules vs per module snapshots with a router.
notes: Demoted 2026-07-16 after review: the move family gets three certified tasks through la8 + ue9 + 3x7 without touching workspace shape. Multi-module is product work for vault-class repos, post freeze, ADR first (workspace identity and load semantics change). Mining single-module repos is the cheaper coverage path meanwhile.

title: Synthetic closure names get a redirect repair
description: GLM resent view backend.func_1 30 times through breaker escalation (vault_cfff8d42): funcN/func_N is gopls's anonymous-function naming, a training prior. Rejection for syms matching (func|funcN|func_N) patterns should say anonymous functions are not addressable and repair to inspect/view of the receiver type. Evidence: run 20260715-223716.

title: Design: workspace transaction graph
description: model-strategies.md closing idea, the flagship exploration: a plan object whose nodes are small verifiable goals (implement interface X -> create type -> methods -> factory -> tests) each gated by a verifier (compile, query assertion, test). The daemon tracks node state; the agent advances only on verified nodes. Shifts 'generate correct code' to 'satisfy small semantic goals' - likely the biggest lever for 9B-30B. New subsystem: DDD design doc first (bounded context, plan aggregate, verifier vocabulary), prototype after project ops.

title: Project invariants checked in the transaction
description: model-strategies.md section 11: machine-readable invariants (no goroutines in pkg X, every exported func takes ctx first, no fmt.Println) validated post-typecheck inside the patch pipeline like verifyResolution, rejecting with the violating positions. Small models obey checklists; the compiler cannot check policy. Design: invariants file format + a small assertion vocabulary over the typed AST.

title: Query kind: package outline
description: model-strategies.md sections 12+14 (workspace contracts, semantic compression): one query that compresses a package to its surface - exported decls with signatures and first doc sentence, grouped by kind. Turns 40 files into one screen; the token-budget lever for small models. All data already in the snapshot.

title: Query kinds: importers and imports
description: model-strategies.md section 2: 'Who imports auth/jwt' with no embeddings. The reverse-importer graph already exists in the engine (rev map, affected()); expose it as query kinds importers {pkg} and imports {pkg}. Cheapest graph-navigation win available.

title: Milestone: SFT corpus generator
description: agent-go-39c concretized: render oracle transcripts as chat-format training episodes; rejected-then-repaired pairs from round episodes as preference data; perturbed oracle patches as rejection-recovery data. One generator, per-format renderers. Gate: after two models of round data exist.

title: Milestone: oracle composition for the last four
description: fb1ea92a + 7103bc2c (func-as-value: set_signature + value-type repairs in one patch), 9354a4eb (interface + implementors set_signature patch; oracle must find the interface method via implementations), b4b95e0f (var-form local promotion). Goal 19/19; each replay recipe becomes SFT corpus material.
notes: DONE 19/19 for the milestone scope: all four blockers certified, full sweep 18/22 with only the four pre-existing move tasks failing (separate effort). Engine: multi-LHS := preflight fix (addparam.go, landed via a1dbb73/8daeb4a interleave), planArgs underscore positional carry (aee7c99). Oracle: bench/compose.go composition module (02884a2), zeroExpr time.Duration. Docs: help note + language.md set_signature row (19d51c8). Evidence: bench/results 20260716-151218 (b4b95e0f), -152559 (9354a4eb), -153244 (fb1ea92a), -153247 (7103bc2c), -154948 (full sweep), commits 118cccc + f8adba6. Not pushed per task instruction.
close_reason: Closed

title: move_decl: members of grouped const/var blocks
description: Oracle: boundary_b26814a3 moves consts declared inside one const(...) group; findDeclRange reports 'declaration not found' for a grouped member. Needs group-aware extraction: slice the single spec line out (plus doc comment), or move the whole group when every member moves. Evidence in bench/results.
close_reason: Grouped members shipped for move_decl (earlier), delete_decl, and upsert_decl (e90c406): single specs excise or replace in place, iota and inherited-value members reject with the blocker named. Proven by the demo tests and the 7ec1fe75 sweep advancing past every grouped reject.

title: move_decl: create missing target package
description: Oracle: boundary_dd2c3807 and vault_7ec1fe75 move decls into packages the ground-truth commit created; move_decl rejects 'target package not found'. Needs upsert-style on-demand package creation, which requires the composable pipeline's created-file path (add_test has one; upsert_decl's composable form documents the ceiling). Evidence in bench/results.
notes: create_pkg shipped with typo-safe repair (2115700, ee98b9c, 18c32a5); dd2c3807 now blocked only on the importFallback stub bug (new bead); 7ec1fe75 blocked on cross-module workspaces (new bead)
close_reason: move_decl creates missing module-local target packages via create_pkg; remaining blockers split into precise beads

title: Mutations blocked by unrelated pre-existing rot
description: Oracle: boundary_eb61ac63 add-param rejects 'affected packages have pre-existing errors' - the preflight refuses to operate when any package in the dirty set already has errors, even unrelated ones. The bench typecheck gate already scores no-NEW-errors against a baseline; the engine preflight should do the same (snapshot the dirty set's existing diagnostics, reject only on new ones after splice) so real-world repos with rot stay editable. Wants a fixture test with a deliberately broken sibling package.
notes: Baseline capture at every preflight, filtered post-splice; parse errors still hard-reject; positions-keyed so shifted rot reads as new (safe direction). Contract change: two tests updated from refusal to tolerance.

title: add_param: ':=' collision at call site
description: Oracle sweep: add-param ctx to CreateNewAccountCli (boundary_57ffc718) rejected 'no new variables on left side of :=' at e2e/boundary/account.go:46. Call-site default insertion appears to interact with := assignments. Needs a minimal repro in the fixture.
notes: Root cause: params share the body scope; ctx := context.Background() in the body redeclared. Matching locals are promoted (line deleted), non-matching collide with a positioned rejection. Verified against boundary 57ffc718^ real path.

title: patch rejects with raw 'stale offset'
description: Episode 20260715-212439 (defense re-run): a patch attempt rejected with reason 'stale offset' - that string is rename's internal splice invariant (rename.go), not a structured Reject with recovery guidance. Reproduce, decide whether it is a real staleness bug in composable patch or an error that should be a structured reject with a re-view repair.
notes: Root cause: GLM sent set_body with statement-op args (at/where/exprs, no body); silent field-dropping let the garbage reach the splice layer where the bounds check surfaced as raw 'stale offset'. Fixed with DisallowUnknownFields on every op decode; foreign args now reject at the shape layer naming the field, with the help-call repair. The splice bounds check remains as defense in depth.

title: Near-miss matching misses renamed symbols
description: Smoke run: after Shared.extractLabels -> extractSwarmLabels, a refs/rename against the old name got no did_you_mean and no repairs; substring matching cannot see extractLabels inside extractSwarmLabels. Add subsequence or edit-distance matching to suggestSymbols so post-rename stale names still produce candidates.

title: Repair filename-as-sym addressing
description: Smoke run 20260715-201514: GLM addressed view with sym=pswarm.go and pswarm.go:232 (filename and file:line), rejected with zero repairs. Emit a redirect repair: strip extension/position, send the search query call for the basename fragment, plus a one-line note that syms are pkg.Sym addresses not files.
notes: Shipped: fileAddressRepair on view and query addressing misses; search call for the file base name.

title: make README code compile. Never gets out of sync.
bench export --mlflow
description: One MLflow run per episode nested under bench run via OTel Go SDK to OTLP /v1/traces. Params profile+mode+harness+task; metrics pass/predicate/wall/tokens/counters; idempotent, one-way from git. Gated on counters existing. Proposal MLflow section.
notes: Raised: publish-intent tenet (docs/tenets.md #12). All evidence is already in git; the exporter makes it queryable/comparable. Build after the qwen floor round so two models' data lands together.
close_reason: MLflow export shipped via REST (no SDK dep): one run per episode, idempotent on episode_path, all identity axes as params, verified 93 runs on the server; sizing-pass aliasing found and fixed in the process

title: Prompt parity for raw mode + token counts in run.json
description: Raw mode gets an equivalently sized workflow paragraph in its own idioms (grep, edit, build). Record both prompts' token counts in run.json (heuristic count fine). Proposal section 6.

title: bench report subcommand
description: Read run dirs, aggregate episodes.jsonl by task/mode/profile: pass@k with binomial CI (Wilson), median time-to-green, capped counts, failure_kind breakdown, counter summaries. Markdown + completion-curve CSV. Proposal section 5.

title: Per-kind goal predicate dispatch
description: Add Kind to runner Manifest (unify with cmd/bench/prep.go copy), map[string]predicateFn, rename logic becomes renamePredicate. add-param/signature/move/wrap-error predicates arrive with their prep extractors. Proposal section 4.

title: Oracle arm as recorded pseudo-mode
description: Oracle harness does not exist in code yet: build it rename-first (replay ground-truth via ago rename per spec), add oracle to the modes loop, score identically. Proves protocol-solvability, gives time-to-green floor, produces SFT corpus. Depends on kind dispatch. Proposal section 4.
notes:
Shipped: oracle pseudo-mode + parallel TestOracleSweep. Definitive map: 11/18 pass (all renames after the baseline test gate, cobra, 2 add-params). 7 add-param failures, every one a set_signature-class ceiling (func-as-value, variadic spread, interface+implementors) plus the := collision bug agent-go-qus. Oracle also caught the add_param import bug and the shared-importer identity bug, both fixed.
Post-fix recount: 13/18 certified. bf1486f7 (spread) and 57ffc718 (:= promotion) flipped tonight. Remaining: fb1ea92a+7103bc2c (func-as-value, need set_signature replay), 9354a4eb (interface, set_signature replay), b4b95e0f (non-matching body local, promotion extension), eb61ac63 (rot preflight agent-go-6sl).
eb61ac63 flipped after rot-tolerant preflights: 14/18 HC + cobra = 15/19 certified. Remaining four all named: func-as-value x2, interface replay, var-form local promotion.

title: Token accounting per episode
description: tokens in/out (+reasoning, cache) and cost per episode. Source: opencode sqlite by session id vs --format json stream; decide on next smoke run; session id capture is prerequisite. Proposal section 3.

title: Bench collects daemon request log per episode
description: Point daemon log at per-workspace path in writeOpencodeConfig/runAgent; record() copies requests.jsonl into epDir. Semantic-mode-only evidence. Depends on daemon -log.

title: Canary probe with restart-on-mismatch (bench)
description: Fixed completion against endpoint before each episode, compared to known-good; on mismatch run AGO_BENCH_RESTART_CMD, re-probe once, record res[canary]. Per-episode cadence first; GLM mid-episode heartbeat deferred. Supersedes agent-go-hao scope for the runner. Proposal section 1.

title: Matrix loop over AGO_BENCH_PROFILES
description: Outer loop over named profiles in BenchmarkRename, profile name in b.Run path. Concurrent endpoints deferred. Proposal section 2.

title: Front coverage: daemon dispatch and CLI verbs
description: internal/snapshot is at 80.9% coverage; the fronts are thin: internal/daemon 23.3%, cmd/ago 37.4%, cmd/bench 0%. All ops route through the daemon switch, so one fixture-driven test walking every verb through the real CLI/daemon path lifts both and guards the routing constraint from the plan. internal/protocol is 20 lines of types, no tests needed.
notes: daemon 54.5->69.1%, cmd/ago 37.4->~51%; TestHandleEveryOp walks the whole switch, TestFlagPlumbing asserts every flag reaches its wire field; buildRequest extracted as the testable seam, reconciled with the README-guard newFlagSet. Zero routing bugs found.

title: Project ops: add_dependency, remove_dependency, move_file, delete_file, mod_tidy
description: The project tier from the spec op table, deferred from the language plan.
close_reason: all five project ops shipped: delete_file, move_file, add_dependency, remove_dependency, mod_tidy; catalog v5

title: README with quickstart and contributing
close_reason: README carries install, quickstart usage block, development rules, and the doc map; drift guarded by readme tests

title: Agent language spec

title: Docs: language for local models (humans and agents)
description: From todo.md: docs aimed at humans and at agents.

title: AGENTS.md common skeleton with per-family variants
description: ago init writes protocol instructions: short, imperative, example-led, one worked query-view-patch-reject-repair loop, explicit no-shell line. One common skeleton with named per-family register variants, not five hand-maintained files. cross-model.md.

title: Devstral 123B decode-speed check
description: Measure real decode t/s at Q4 on the 128GB Mac before any 123B bench run; the number decides k=3 vs k=1 for the adversarial cell. Gates the Devstral 123B roster row. devstral.md / README open item.

title: Prompt prefix hygiene
description: No worktree paths, task ids, or dates in system prompt or tool catalog; stable tool ordering in tools/list; byte-stable help per catalog version. Lets k=3 episodes share a warm cache. cross-model.md bench #4.

title: Harness resend audit
description: Per-stack checklists: tool results resent byte-identical, results on the tool role (never a second user message), reasoning-block handling per model rule. cross-model.md bench #5.

title: Canary probe with restart-on-mismatch
description: Fixed probe completion before each episode checked against known-good output; restart server on failure. Motivated by GLM wedge-while-healthy; health-check-lies is a risk on every stack. cross-model.md bench #3.
close_reason: canary.go shipped: fixed probe before episodes, known-good comparison, AGO_BENCH_RESTART_CMD restart on mismatch

title: Help examples validated against fixture
description: Every catalog entry carries one minimal patch accepted against the fixture. Task 12 asserts examples parse; extend to would-validate. cross-model.md engine #8.
notes: TestHelpExamplesAcceptedByFixture dry-runs every catalog example; 21/26 were broken (nonexistent syms, out-of-scope idents, ceiling-violating examples). All rewritten to real fixture targets; multi-op examples (1..3, last op is the entry) for setup-needing ops.

title: Unknown-tool/op aliases and no-shell redirect
description: Alias obvious name variants (ago_view, get_view); answer hallucinated shell-ish tools (bash, str_replace, execute_command) with one-line redirect: no shell here, mutations go through patch ops. cross-model.md engine #7.

title: Resubmission circuit breaker
description: Hash rejected patches; exact resend of a just-rejected patch gets an escalated rejection: shorter reason, harder imperative naming the recovery call. cross-model.md engine #6.
notes: Shipped daemon-wide, not patch-only: resendBreaker escalates any exact resend of a rejected request with resent count and hard imperative. Evidence: episode 20260715-210756 resent one rejected view 23 times.

title: Lenient patch decode, repair noted
description: Accept literal newlines and tabs inside JSON strings, trailing commas, single-quoted keys; report the normalization in the response. Absorbs the most common damage class from small models in code-bearing string args. cross-model.md engine #5.

title: Shrink the multi-file write window: stage temp files, then rename-commit
description: Attribution of agent-go-0cm showed accepted multi-file ops (add_param with 62-88 call sites) expose half-written on-disk states while os.WriteFile loops run; the frozen qwen round sampled 5 such states in the semantic arm. True cross-file atomicity is impossible with plain files, but writing all temps first and then renaming shrinks the window from full write time to N renames. Lands with a test that interleaves a reader with a large multi-file patch commit.

title: SSA-backed call graph: give go/ssa a consumer or strike the claim
description: idea.md claims the engine sits on go/packages, go/types, go/ssa, and gopls machinery; go/ssa has zero imports in the tree. callers/callees run on go/types, so calls through interfaces report the interface method rather than concrete reachable targets. Give SSA a consumer: build x/tools/go/ssa over the snapshot and run RTA or VTA so callers through an interface can enumerate concrete implementations actually reachable, with a dead query kind falling out nearly free. Needs design for rebuild cost: SSA over a big workspace is not per-mutation cheap, so it likely builds lazily per query with generation-keyed invalidation. Until this lands, the idea.md architecture line overstates; either land this or strike go/ssa from the claim (paper honesty, unrepresentable-not-checked framing).
design: Candidates: golang.org/x/tools/go/ssa + go/callgraph/rta (cheap, needs roots; fine for main+tests) or vta (no roots, pricier). Surface as query kinds: callers gains concrete=true results through interfaces; new kind dead. Lazy build on first SSA query, invalidated by generation bump; never in the mutation path.
notes: Supersedes agent-go-07u (SSA query tier, deferred from the language plan): same intent, this issue adds the concrete design (RTA/VTA, lazy generation-keyed build, callers concrete=true, dead kind) and the idea.md honesty angle.

title: Episode records pass=true with failure_kind=oracle_reject (cobra_0960ff7f)
description: cobra_0960ff7f oracle episodes score pass=true but carry failure_kind=oracle_reject in every sweep since at least 20260716-175904: one oracle call errors yet the end state satisfies predicate+typecheck+tests. Either the replay makes a redundant call that rejects, or classifyFailure should not stamp a passing episode. Decide which side is lying and fix; evidence is misleading as recorded.
close_reason: Root cause was vacuous evidence, not just recording: cobra_0960ff7f is a pre-modules commit whose empty-pkg spec zeroed every count. Fixed at four layers (a793753): HasSpecs validation, certify revocation, zero-baseline predicate guard, extractor drop; failure_kind no longer stamps passing episodes. Task revoked via bench certify; dev set is 20.

title: Decl tier stubs: remove_param sugar, implement_interface, add_bench
description: The three UNSHIPPED rows left from agent-go-596 after move_decl and set_signature shipped. remove_param is sugar over set_signature (which already drops params); implement_interface generates missing method stubs; add_bench is the BenchmarkXxx skeleton. Each is small; language.md rows already document them as UNSHIPPED and TestLanguageSpecOpRowsMatchRegistry forces the row flip on ship.

title: Prometheus metrics integration
description: Expose operational metrics: daemon side (requests by op/outcome, rejection reasons, repair offers and uptake, retypecheck duration and dirty-set size, snapshot load time, generation churn) and bench side (episode outcomes, wall times, token totals) as Prometheus metrics. Daemon gets an optional metrics listener (off by default, env-gated like AGO_LOG_REQUESTS); the request log stays the source of truth for per-episode evidence - metrics are the live-operations view, complementing the MLflow export (agent-go-js2.14) which owns post-hoc analysis. Fits the bench proposal's Grafana note for live rounds.

title: Test-failure responses carry next calls
description: model-strategies.md section 5 (test-guided repair): the test tool's per-failure output should carry possible_next - the view call for the failing test function and for the deepest same-module frame in its output. Same paste-ready philosophy as rejection repairs, applied to red tests.

title: Speculative dry-run pattern
description: model-strategies.md section 8 (multiple candidates) is protocol-native here: dry_run makes candidate edits nearly free. Teach generate-N-dry_run-commit-best in AGENTS.md and measure it as a bench arm. Harness/prompt work, no engine change.

title: Query kind: history (previous successful edits)
description: model-strategies.md section 9: 'last commit touching this API' via git log -L on the decl range. Humans do this constantly; models should be able to ask for it. Needs git in the loop - decide whether the daemon shells out or the harness owns it.

title: Daemonless cold start: content-hash snapshot cache
description: Experiment 2026-07-16: parallel retypecheck landed (273ms->56ms on boundary 117-pkg dirty set) but cold packages.Load stays ~2s warm-cache. Dropping NeedDeps (deps from export data) cut cold to 1.3s but mixes type universes on incremental splice (root-to-root imports from separate export sessions) - reverted, evidence in commit history. The sound path: persist workspace packages' export data keyed by content hash (gopls filecache approach); cold start = load cache + parallel recheck of changed packages only. Would make one-shot CLI use viable without the daemon.

title: Concurrency vocabulary: mine tasks, then ops
description: Catalog has add_go/add_defer only; select, channel send, and sync idioms are deliberately deferred to set_body per language.md, gated on bench evidence. But no concurrency tasks are mined, so evidence cannot appear. Unlock: mine a concurrency task kind (commits adding mutex guards, converting serial loops to errgroup/bounded concurrency), oracle-validate, then judge candidate ops on the results: wrap_stmts with:go, guard_with_mutex, channel send statement, async edge flag on callers/callees. Query-side (what does this mutex guard) belongs to the SSA tier (agent-go-07u).

title: Harness axis: record + Pi driver
description: res[harness] label first. Then driver interface (opencode|pi), Pi via MCP-server extension route, purity flags, thinking dial, token extraction from Pi session JSONL. Sequenced late: rename round, one model first. Proposal Harness section.

title: Contamination caveat in bench.md
description: Mode-vs-mode comparison holds contamination constant; it inflates absolute rates; recency-mining is the escape hatch. Doc-only. Proposal section 7.

title: SSA query tier
description: Queries are static-only in v1. Includes callers/callees candidate expansion through method sets (dc65d0f). Deferred from the language plan.

title: Handle migration on stale generations
description: Today a moved generation forces re-view; migrate handles across generations where the decl body is unchanged. Deferred from the language plan.

title: Structured expression nodes
description: Grammar frozen in language.md (ident/select/index/call/lit/unary/binary/paren/func); v1 accepts text atoms only. Target for constrained decoding: one JSON grammar covers a whole patch. Consumers are the per-stack grammar integrations; nothing universal waits on it (cross-model.md non-builds).

title: Make it pretty
description: From todo.md; scope undefined.

title: MiMo-V2-Flash optimization doc
description: Borderline roster candidate (309B/15B active, MTP, claims top open-source SWE-bench Verified). Same size class as DeepSeek V4 Flash; viable only via aggressive selective quant. Write the doc if community quants prove out at ~80GB. optimizations/README.md.

title: Oracle harness as training-data generator
description: Oracle solutions rendered as episode transcripts give SFT data; rejected-then-repaired pairs give preference data; perturbed oracle patches give rejection-recovery data. Rendering differs per chat format; generator is one piece of work. cross-model.md long game.

title: No markdown fences in protocol output
description: Plain text everywhere in views, help, rejections; models mirror formatting. Grep shows none today; add a guard test so it stays true. cross-model.md engine #9.
close_reason: TestNoMarkdownFencesInProtocolOutput guards help, views, and rejections; grep was already clean, now it stays that way
